One adapter instance controls one wallbo: One adapter instance controls one wallbox. Create multiple instances if you have multiple chargers.
auto = try local first, then cloud fallb: auto = try local first, then cloud fallback
If no host is configured, the adapter fi: If no host is configured, the adapter first tries UDP broadcast and then a TCP subnet scan.
Query cloud charge records (today / tota: Query cloud charge records (today / total energy)
Debug: log local raw frame sizes: Debug: log local raw frame sizes
Older SmartCharge models can usually be: Older SmartCharge models can usually be found via UDP broadcast (48890 → 48899) and controlled locally on TCP port 9988. If UDP does not answer, the adapter also scans the local subnet for TCP 9988.
Automatic discovery works on adapter sta: Automatic discovery works on adapter start if no host is configured. For a manual search in Admin, save and start the instance once, then use the search button or the host dropdown.
Select a discovered charger or enter the: Select a discovered charger or enter the host manually. If the IP changes, the adapter can rediscover the wallbox automatically.
19-digit device ID; leave empty if you w: 19-digit device ID; leave empty if you want the adapter to read it from the wallbox.
Usually 0.0.0.0. Change only if you need: Usually 0.0.0.0. Change only if you need to bind discovery to a specific IPv4 address.
Used for UDP discovery and as the base t: Used for UDP discovery and as the base timeout before the subnet fallback starts.
Cloud mode targets the X-Cheng backend u: Cloud mode targets the X-Cheng backend used by DS Charge / SmartCharge app families. It is mainly useful for app-bound older chargers where local TCP is not reachable.
Enabled by default because community int: Enabled by default because community integrations disable strict TLS validation against this backend.
Leave empty to auto-select the first clo: Leave empty to auto-select the first cloud device.
Local TCP config values are write-only o: Local TCP config values are write-only on the wallbox. The adapter mirrors the last written values in charger.control.* states so your EMS can keep a stable desired setpoint.
Number of consecutive local poll failure: Number of consecutive local poll failures before auto mode switches to cloud.
Local TCP config values are write-only o: Local TCP config values are write-only on the wallbox. The adapter mirrors the last written values in charger.control.* states so your EMS can keep a stable desired setpoint. Values below 6 A trigger a stop command instead of sending an unsupported current to the wallbox.
Used when the wallbox reports voltage/cu: Used when the wallbox reports voltage/current but no reliable power value. Set 1 for 7.2 kW single-phase chargers and 3 for 11/22 kW chargers.
